location.office_time_required: Monday through Thursday in-office weekly (four days/week), with no required office days during summer and winter holidays

secondary_archetype: Platform / Infrastructure PM managing multiple PM teams

seniority.level: Staff / Group PM (people manager of PMs)
level_signals: Player/coach role managing PMs embedded in trading and market data domains
Responsible for multi-year vision and executive-level strategic discussions
Comp range $187K-$214K base consistent with senior/group PM at fintech
Oversees product teams across multiple domains simultaneously

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Product management experience at a financial services or financial technology company with a focus on back-end systems and applications
Deep expertise in and passion for financial concepts such as portfolio management, order routing, trade execution workflows, FIX protocol, and securities data management
Strong storytelling skills to distill advanced Broker/Dealer concepts into accessible information for partner organizations, stakeholders, and executives alike
Technical fluency: able to engage meaningfully with engineers on systems design, data modeling, and architectural tradeoffs
Experience managing or mentoring other PMs, with a track record of growing product talent and maintaining strategic coherence across a multi-team domain
Creative problem-solving skills to deliver innovative functionality within regulatory and technical constraints.
A self-starter with proven success in launching products in collaboration with a broad set of stakeholders

scope.surface: Trading and market data systems underpinning $55B+ in client assets across retail, advised, and 401(k) lines of business; includes Trade Execution, Portfolio Management, Security Master, and Market Data infrastructure
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B financial services (retail investing, RIA/advised clients, SMB 401(k))
primary_metrics_inferred: Platform reliability and uptime for mission-critical trading systems
Automation rate / reduction in manual workflows
Speed and accuracy of trade execution
Revenue-enabling product launches across three lines of business
core_responsibilities: Oversee product teams responsible for trading and market data functions
Define the multi-year vision for platform evolution including new asset class support, real-time data expansion, and security classification models
Partner with engineering leadership to balance roadmap priorities across retail, advised, and 401(k) lines of business
Manage PMs embedded in trading and market data domains, providing strategic direction and development feedback
Develop success metrics, tracking plans, and communicate impact of prioritized work
Collaborate with Capital Markets, Custody, Investing, Operations, and Legal & Compliance on complex client and regulatory situations
Support engineering teams during standups, planning meetings, and retros

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($187K-$214K base) plus competitive equity package
Clear product surface and ownership scope (Trade Execution, Portfolio Management, Security Master, Market Data)
Specific AUM context ($55B+ in client assets) signals scale and organizational maturity
Explicit player/coach framing clarifies management expectations upfront
Hybrid policy details are transparent (Mon-Thu in-office, holiday flexibility)
Broad benefits package including equity, health, dental, vision, 401(k) match, flexible PTO
red_flags: Four days/week in-office requirement (Mon-Thu) is more intensive than most hybrid roles — fully remote candidates excluded
No years-of-experience minimum stated, making it hard to self-qualify seniority expectations
Visa sponsorship not addressed in JD body; E-Verify participation noted which may signal limitations for non-citizens
Nice-to-haves section is entirely absent — all qualifications presented as must-haves with no softer fallback

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from player/coach framing, multi-team ownership, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not in JD)
location.visa_sponsorship (not stated; E-Verify participation noted but no explicit sponsorship policy)
ambiguities: No explicit 'nice to have' or 'preferred' section exists in the JD; all items under 'What we're looking for' are treated as hard must-haves.
Visa sponsorship policy is not addressed; E-Verify is mentioned which implies work authorization verification but does not confirm or deny sponsorship availability — marked as 'unclear'.

Role title says 'Group Product Manager' but JD alternately uses 'Group Product Manager' framing implying people management — confirmed as people manager from 'manage PM(s)' language in responsibilities.
